name: PsExec Process Masquerading
notes: This rule triggers when PsExec IMP Hash is detected for another process name.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event QID is one of the following (5001828) Process Create
when the event matches IMP Hash (custom) is any of [159D56D406180A332FBC99290F30700E or C1E59519B5E5D84AF07AFA6F5A8625F1]
when the event matches Process Name NOT IMATCHES 'PsExec(\d\d)?\.exe' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY PsExec Process Masquerading on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event QID is one of the following (5001828) Process Create
AND when the event matches IMP Hash (custom) is any of [159D56D406180A332FBC99290F30700E or C1E59519B5E5D84AF07AFA6F5A8625F1]
AND when the event matches Process Name NOT IMATCHES 'PsExec(\d\d)?\.exe' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Detected PsExec with a Different Process Name
Event description: Detected PsExec with a Different Process Name
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
Add the "sourceIP" of the event or flow payload to the Reference Set "Compromised Hosts"
